hero.subtitle: Authorization Code Exchange for web and mobile — Meta app setup, API reference, and frontend examples.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Facebook login is optional and controlled by FACEBOOK_AUTH_ENABLED. When disabled, Facebook endpoints return 503 Service Unavailable. JWTs are never placed in the redirect URL; the API issues a one-time exchangeCode (Redis, 60 seconds). The same POST /auth/exchange endpoint is shared with Google.
items: Same token response shape as POST /auth/login

console.title: Meta for Developers
console.description: Create a Facebook App with Facebook Login and set the Valid OAuth Redirect URI to match FACEBOOK_CALLBACK_URL.
steps: Open Meta for Developers → My Apps → Create App
Under Facebook Login → Settings, add Valid OAuth Redirect URI (e.g. http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/facebook/callback)
Copy App ID and App Secret into your .env

env.title: Environment variables
env.description: Loaded from .env via src/config/facebook-oauth.config.ts (namespace facebookOAuth on ConfigService). See .env.example.

roles.title: Default roles for new Facebook users
roles.description: New Facebook users receive roles: [] by default (same as password register). Set FACEBOOK_OAUTH_DEFAULT_ROLES in .env to a comma-separated list of Role values (e.g. user or user,manager). Existing users linked by email keep their current roles.

flow.title: Sequence
items: User consents on Facebook; browser hits GET /api/v1/auth/facebook/callback
API upserts user, stores exchangeCode in Redis, redirects to redirect?code=...
Client POSTs { code } to /api/v1/auth/exchange and receives JWTs

start.title: 1. Start OAuth
start.description: Opens Facebook consent. Query redirect must match a prefix in FACEBOOK_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST (or omit to use the first allowlist entry).

callback.title: 2. Callback
callback.description: Handled by the API. Redirects to your app with ?code= (or &code= if the URL already has a query).

exchange.title: 3. Exchange code
exchange.description: Public endpoint shared with Google. Consumes the code (single use) and returns the same shape as login.

web.title: Frontend — Web (SPA)
web.description: Typical flow: button starts OAuth in the same window (or popup); a callback route on your frontend origin reads code from the query string and calls exchange.
notes: Add your frontend callback URL to FACEBOOK_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST (e.g. http://localhost:5173/auth/callback)
FACEBOOK_CALLBACK_URL must point at the Nest API, not the SPA
Store accessToken / refreshToken securely (memory + httpOnly cookie patterns as you prefer)

mobile.title: Frontend — Mobile (deep link)
mobile.description: Use a custom scheme (or universal link) in the allowlist. After Facebook, the API redirects to myapp://success?code=.... The app opens, parses the code, and calls exchange.
notes: Register the scheme in the OS / Expo app.json
Exchange over HTTPS against your API base URL

security.title: Security notes
items: Redirect URLs must match FACEBOOK_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST prefixes
Exchange codes are single-use and expire in 60 seconds (Redis)
Existing local users are linked by verified Facebook email (facebookId set; roles unchanged)
Facebook-only users have password null — password login / change-password will fail until a password is set
Email permission is required; login fails if Facebook does not return an email

userModel.title: User model
userModel.description: Postgres entity / interface fields used by Facebook login:
items: facebookId — unique nullable Facebook subject ID
isActive — set true for new Facebook users

samples.fullPageRedirect: // Full-page redirect to Nest → Facebook → back to SPA
samples.persistTokens: // persist tokens as your app requires
